name: tailwindcss
summary: Utility-first CSS framework — compose designs directly in markup with small, single-purpose class names.

story.problem: Writing CSS means constantly context-switching between HTML and CSS files, naming things (what do you call this div?), and fighting specificity wars. Component-scoped CSS still means maintaining a separate styling system. Designs drift as CSS grows.
story.mental_model: Tailwind replaces CSS files with a vocabulary of utility classes directly in your HTML. Instead of `.card { padding: 1rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; }` you write `class='p-4 rounded-lg'`. The design system (spacing, colors, typography) is defined once in config — every class enforces it.
story.when_to_use: Any web project where you control the markup. Especially powerful with component frameworks (React, Vue, Svelte). Rapid prototyping, design systems, teams that want consistency without writing CSS.
story.when_not_to_use: When you don't control the HTML (CMS-generated content). Projects with existing CSS codebases where a migration isn't worth it. Developers who strongly prefer semantic CSS.

alternatives.name: CSS Modules
alternatives.reason: Scoped CSS files without the utility class approach. Less opinionated.

alternatives.name: styled-components
alternatives.reason: CSS-in-JS, keeps styles co-located with components.

alternatives.name: UnoCSS
alternatives.reason: Tailwind-compatible utility engine but faster and more customizable.

alternatives.name: Bootstrap
alternatives.reason: Component-first, pre-styled. Faster for prototypes, less flexible for custom designs.

nodes.label: tailwindcss
nodes.summary: PostCSS plugin and CLI. `@import 'tailwindcss'` in CSS or use CLI.

nodes.label: tailwind.config.js
nodes.summary: Customize theme (colors, spacing, fonts), extend with new values, add plugins. v4 uses CSS config.

nodes.label: Utility classes
nodes.summary: p-4, m-2, text-lg, font-bold, flex, grid, bg-blue-500, text-white, rounded-xl, shadow-md, etc.

nodes.label: Responsive prefixes
nodes.summary: sm:, md:, lg:, xl: — `class='text-sm md:text-base lg:text-lg'`. Mobile-first breakpoints.

nodes.label: State variants
nodes.summary: hover:, focus:, active:, disabled:, dark:, group-hover: — `hover:bg-blue-700`.

nodes.label: @apply directive
nodes.summary: Extract repeated utility combinations into CSS class: `.btn { @apply px-4 py-2 rounded font-bold; }`.

nodes.label: Plugins (typography, forms, animate)
nodes.summary: @tailwindcss/typography for prose, @tailwindcss/forms for styled form elements, tailwindcss-animate.

tasks.title: Install Tailwind CSS v4 in a Next.js project

steps.code:

  Click me
</button>
